worth_reading.title: Better tools made Copilot code review worse. Here’s how we actually improved it.
worth_reading.author: GitHub Copilot
worth_reading.summary: How migrating Copilot code review to shared Unix-style code exploration tools reduced review cost by reshaping agent workflows around pull request evidence. signal_watch.title: You can now deploy Lovable apps to Vercel
signal_watch.author: Vercel AI
signal_watch.summary: Vercel now supports Lovable applications with zero configuration. To get started, sync your Lovable project to GitHub , then import the repository to Vercel. Vercel automatically detects the framework ( TanStack Start ) and deploys your app. Once connected, every change you make in Lovable syncs to GitHub and triggers a new deployment on Vercel. Lovable projects now use Nitro under the hood. Nitro is the universal server toolkit that powers zero-config deployments for TanStac
signal_watch.excerpt: Vercel now supports Lovable applications with zero configuration. To get started, sync your Lovable project to GitHub , then import the repository to Vercel. Vercel automatically detects the framework ( TanStack Start ) and deploys your app. Once connected, every change you make in Lovable syncs to GitHub and triggers a new deployment on Vercel. Lovable projects now use Nitro under the hood. Nitro is the universal server toolkit that powers zero-config deployments for TanStac

signal_watch.title: Delta Channels: How We’re Evolving our Runtime for Long-Running Agents
signal_watch.author: LangChain
signal_watch.summary: Long-running agents have a storage problem: checkpointing full state at every step grows at O(N²). DeltaChannel is a new primitive in LangGraph 1.2 that checkpoints only the diff each step and writes full snapshots periodically, keeping storage costs flat as sessions grow longer. It ships by default in Deep Agents v0.6, with no config changes or data migration required.
signal_watch.excerpt: Long-running agents have a storage problem: checkpointing full state at every step grows at O(N²). DeltaChannel is a new primitive in LangGraph 1.2 that checkpoints only the diff each step and writes full snapshots periodically, keeping storage costs flat as sessions grow longer. It ships by default in Deep Agents v0.6, with no config changes or data migration required.
signal_watch.reason_selected: 当日新抓取且未展示过：Delta Channels: How We’re Evolving our Runtime for Long-Running Agents 来自 LangChain 的公开抓取入口，证据链保留原始链接与抓取来源。

DeltaChannel is a new primitive in LangGraph 1.2 that checkpoints only the diff each step and writes full snapshots periodically, keeping storage costs flat as sessions grow longer. It ships by default in Deep Agents v0.6, with no config changes or data migration required.

clusters.title: Better tools made Copilot code review worse. Here’s how we actually improved it.

The post Better tools made Copilot code review worse. Here’s how we actually improved it. appeared first on The GitHub Blog .

clusters.title: Accelerating End-to-End Co-Folding Performance with NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit
clusters.summary: NVIDIA Technical Blog · Accelerating End-to-End Co-Folding Performance with NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit

clusters.title: You can now deploy Lovable apps to Vercel
clusters.summary: Vercel now supports Lovable applications with zero configuration. To get started, sync your Lovable project to GitHub , then import the repository to Vercel. Vercel automatically detects the framework ( TanStack Start ) and deploys your app. Once connected, every change you make in Lovable syncs to GitHub and triggers a new deployment on Vercel. Lovable projects now use Nitro under the hood. Nitro is the universal server toolkit that powers zero-config deployments for TanStac

clusters.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI

clusters.title: OpenWiki Brains: Proactive Memory for AI Agents
clusters.summary: OpenWiki Brains turns sources like Gmail, Notion, Git, X, Hacker News, and web search into a local wiki that agents can use as fresh, proactive memory.

clusters.summary: LangSmith Evaluation now includes 30+ evaluator templates and a central hub to reuse evaluators across projects — so you can ship better evals faster without starting from scratch each time.

clusters.title: How we build evals for Deep Agents
clusters.summary: Build targeted evals for Deep Agents that measure real behaviors. Discover data curation, metrics, and testing strategies to improve agent accuracy.

clusters.title: Regression Testing with LangSmith
clusters.summary: Evaluate and iterate on LLM applications with confidence using LangSmith's regression testing. Compare experiments, track performance, and identify changes.

clusters.title: Improving Memory Retrieval: How New Computer achieved 50% higher recall with LangSmith
clusters.summary: New Computer used LangSmith to improve their memory retrieval system, achieving 50% higher recall by tracking regressions in comparison view and adjusting conversation prompts accordingly.

clusters.title: Pushing LangSmith to new limits with Replit Agent's complex workflows
clusters.summary: See how Replit built their agents atop LangGraph and integrated LangSmith to pinpoint issues, improve the performance of their agents, and enable human-in-the-loop workflows.

clusters.title: Introducing OpenTelemetry support for LangSmith
clusters.summary: LangSmith now supports OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing and observability.

clusters.title: Introducing Align Evals: Streamlining LLM Application Evaluation
clusters.summary: Align Evals is a new feature in LangSmith that helps you calibrate your evaluators to better match human preferences.

clusters.title: Evaluating Deep Agents: Our Learnings
clusters.summary: Learn 5 patterns for evaluating deep agents: bespoke testing, single-step validation, full turns, multi-turn simulations, and environment setup.

clusters.title: monday Service + LangSmith: Building a Code-First Evaluation Strategy from Day 1
clusters.summary: Learn how monday Service developed an eval-driven development framework for their customer-facing service agents.

clusters.title: Full Text Search in SmithDB: Designing an Inverted Index for Object Storage
clusters.summary: SmithDB supports full-text search and JSON filtering over agent traces with a median (P50) latency of 400 ms, even though the underlying data consists of large, deeply nested JSON documents stored in object storage.

clusters.title: Delta Channels: How We’re Evolving our Runtime for Long-Running Agents
clusters.summary: Long-running agents have a storage problem: checkpointing full state at every step grows at O(N²). DeltaChannel is a new primitive in LangGraph 1.2 that checkpoints only the diff each step and writes full snapshots periodically, keeping storage costs flat as sessions grow longer. It ships by default in Deep Agents v0.6, with no config changes or data migration required.

clusters.title: Interpreter Skills: Building Workflows for Agents
